# 2026-02-20

## LinkedIn Setup
- Logged into Dwayne's LinkedIn (dwayne@urbanbutter.com) via openclaw browser

- Old password was accidentally shared in group chat — deleted
- Profile still shows "President and CEO at Urbanbutter Creative Group inc." — Dwayne updating
- Published first SolveWorks post (origin story + what SolveWorks does + link)
- Plan: 3-4 posts/week going forward

## SolveWorks Leads
- Mike from Rylem (staffing agency) — strong lead, asking implementation questions
- Wants auto-extraction of leads matching criteria + automated outreach sequence
- Dwayne sent response about ICP-based auto-prospecting
- Leads coming from Darryl's YPO network — NOT just small business anymore
- Updated LinkedIn copy to be size-agnostic (removed "small business" framing)

## SolveWorks X Account — LIVE
- Handle: @Solveworks_io

- Bio set, display name changed to "SolveWorks", website linked
- Still needs: profile pic (SolveWorks logo), banner image
- Posting autonomously — brand voice, AI/business content

- Committed: YPO one-pager, LinkedIn posts, 7 Cellars private event research, SolveWorks data

## Mission Control Dashboard
- **Concept:** From YouTube video about OpenClaw Mission Control (Aj6hoC9JaLI)
- **Decision:** Build as static pages on solveworks.io (GitHub Pages), NOT local hosting or Vercel
- **URL pattern:** solveworks.io/mission/ (Mika), solveworks.io/7cellars/ (partners), solveworks.io/clientname/ (future clients)
- **Architecture:** Pure HTML/CSS/JS, password-protected, JSON data synced every 5 min from Mac Mini
- **7 Cellars dashboard:** Next build after Mission Control — includes kanban (replaces Trello), comments/notes for partners, inventory, sales, meeting summaries
- **SolveWorks product:** Every client gets their own dashboard — key retention play and deliverable
- **Sub-agent building it now:** mission-control-static

## Auto-checkpoint (10:57 AM)
- Committed: HeyGen avatar video, 7 Cellars dashboard, Mission Control dashboard (17 files)
- Pushed to origin, backed up private files

## Auto-checkpoint (3:57 PM)

- Pushed to origin, backed up private files

## Auto-checkpoint (2:57 PM)
- Committed: one-pager-v2 (HTML+PDF), solveworks-data updates, mission control memory sync
- Pushed to origin, backed up private files

## Auto-checkpoint (12:57 PM)
- Committed: 22 files — demo dashboard, Ace Upholstery proposal, 7 Cellars dashboard updates, Cin7 orders data, meetings data, DECISIONS.md + MEMORY.md updates
- Pushed to origin, backed up private files

## Dashboards Built Today
- **solveworks.io/mission/** — Mika's Mission Control (dark theme, password: mika2026)
- **solveworks.io/7cellars/** — Partner dashboard (light theme, password: 7cellars2026, kanban, inventory, meetings, sales)
- **solveworks.io/demo/** — SolveWorks sales demo (no password, fake data, for pitching clients)
- All static HTML/CSS/JS on GitHub Pages — no frameworks, no build step
- Architecture: JSON data files synced every 5 min from Mac Mini
- Decision: All client dashboards live as routes on solveworks.io
- Decision: Always use 7 Cellars bottle-only logo (saved to DECISIONS.md)
- Cin7 inventory has no stock data — use Shopify for stock levels
- 234 products loaded, 27 meeting summaries, 14 tasks
- Next: Shared task backend so partners see same kanban, Cin7 wholesale sales when orders start

## TODO: Testimonials for SolveWorks
- **Drew Millington** — Raving review, says "it changed his life." Need his actual quote. Remind Dwayne to get it.
- **Darryl (Revaly)** — Also offered to give a testimonial. Need his actual words too.
- Once received: Add to one-pager, proposals, and website.

# 2026-02-19 (Wednesday)

## Auto-checkpoints
- 04:57 — Committed 2 new research files (revenue-ideas, website-audits) and pushed. Private backup ran.
- 05:57 — Created today's memory file. No uncommitted changes.

## McAdoo's Lead — Tyler
- Email: tylert@mcadoos.ca
- AI Audit score: 85/100, 21 hrs/week savings potential
- Key pain points: manual customer comms, 10+ hrs/week admin, no data tracking, team resistance to new tools
- Proposal being generated (no pricing yet — Brody doing the call)
- **IMPORTANT for final proposal:** Include AI-powered proposal generation as a solution — Tyler's team already uses templates for proposals, automate that. Dwayne flagged this specifically.

## LESSON: Proposal Projections Must Be Realistic
- Revaly proposal had savings projections HIGHER than their actual expenses — killed credibility
- Darryl's feedback: "lost credibility then built it" because of inaccurate numbers
- **Rules going forward:**
  1. NEVER fabricate or estimate savings without real data from a discovery call
  2. If no discovery call data, use conservative ranges or say "to be determined after discovery"
  3. Better to underestimate than overestimate — credibility > impressive numbers
  4. Always have a discovery call before final proposal
  5. Don't hallucinate revenue/expense/savings figures
- This applies to ALL proposals, not just Revaly

## Revaly Discovery Call — Next Week
- Brody doing discovery call with one of the Revaly owners
- Building a personal agent (like Drew's setup)
- Use the new discovery template for this call
- Remember: previous Revaly proposal had inflated numbers — this time we get REAL data from the call
- Darryl owns Revaly — every interaction is a SolveWorks demo

## SolveWorks Pricing — CONFIRMED
- **Setup:** $1,500 (one-time)
- **Monthly:** $250/month (maintenance — new skills, updates, support)
- Source: Dwayne's friend pitched to YPO group, multiple interested, this pricing suggested
- YPO members run businesses $5M+ revenue
- Multiple booking calls coming in from this

## SolveWorks Pricing — UPDATED
- **Setup:** $1,500 (one-time)
- **Option A:** $250/month — client pays their own Anthropic API costs
- **Option B:** $450/month — fully managed, SolveWorks covers AI/API costs
- Both include: maintenance, new skills, updates, priority support

## Case Studies — Real Clients
- Replace old fictional case studies with real ones
- **Dwayne S.** — 3 businesses (urbanbutter, 7 Cellars, SolveWorks) with one agent
- **Anthony M.** (Drew) — Senior exec, personal agent deployment
- Wait a few days then ask Anthony for a quote
- Follow up: ~Feb 22-23 for Anthony's quote

## SolveWorks — Pricing in USD
- All pricing is USD standard
- Exceptions case by case at Dwayne's discretion

## SolveWorks Deployment Model — CONFIRMED
- Mac Mini fleet model: buy, build here, ship to client, plug and play
- $1,500 setup (covers Mini ~$600 + build time)
- $250/month — client pays own Anthropic API key
- $450/month — fully managed, SolveWorks covers API costs
- All pricing in USD
- Kill switch via Tailscale + SSH + OpenClaw config control
- Every Mini ships with: Tailscale, SSH keys, auto-start, SolveWorks-controlled config
- Client just plugs in ethernet + power
- Remote management via Tailscale for updates, skills, troubleshooting

## Proposals Sent — Feb 19
- **McAdoo's (Tyler)** — tylert@mcadoos.ca — construction/landscaping
- **Rylem (Mike)** — miked@rylem.com — Seattle staffing agency  
- **HitLights (Joe)** — jfreeman@hitlights.com — LED lighting products, 5K+ installers
- All pre-call proposals, framed as initial assessments
- All came from YPO network via Darryl's friend
- Discovery calls booked with Brody

## First Inbound SolveWorks Lead! 🔥 (~10:30 AM)
- Lead came in minutes after Dwayne posted the "Beyond Chatbots" blog to Facebook
- Lead is in real estate, brother has a welding company
- Booked a call via Calendly
- Info being shared with Brody
- Blog post → Facebook → lead in under an hour. Content marketing WORKS.

## Day 2 Progress (~6 AM - 11 AM)

### SolveWorks
- Blog post "Beyond Chatbots" published to solveworks.io ✅
- Had wrong styling (white bg) — restyled to match dark theme. LESSON: always check existing templates first.
- Dwayne posted to Facebook → FIRST INBOUND LEAD within minutes! Real estate guy, brother has welding company. Booked a Calendly call.
- Info shared with Brody

### Infrastructure
- Created DECISIONS.md — all confirmed decisions in one place
- Created ERRORS.md — lessons learned, never repeat
- Updated AGENTS.md boot sequence to include both files
- Context switching between chats was losing recent work — fix: write to memory in real-time, every session reads DECISIONS.md + ERRORS.md + today's memory
- Anthropic API had a transient 500 error — resolved itself

### Overnight Jobs (confirmed working)

- Morning briefing compiled and delivered to Dwayne's DM at 6 AM ✅

- 6:57 PM: Push to origin FAILED — 7cellars/images.zip (101MB) exceeds GitHub 100MB limit. Needs removal from history or .gitignore + LFS. Backup script ran OK.
- Evening: More work done — Vivino scores for 7 Cellars wines, SolveWorks pricing research (md+pdf), SolveWorks docs (deployment security, managed agent architecture, pricing tiers)
- Skipping git push until images.zip resolved; private backup running fine

## Revaly Enterprise Security Blueprint (~2-5 PM)
- Created branded PDF responding to Darryl's security concerns about OpenClaw
- Used SolveWorks HTML proposal template (revaly-proposal.html as base)

- Files: `solveworks-site/proposals/revaly-security-blueprint.html` + `.pdf` (690KB)
- Pushed to GitHub: `dwayne-ubcg/solveworks` repo

- **Google Doc attempt rejected by Dwayne** — plain doc format unacceptable, must use branded HTML template
- Dwayne flagged process inefficiency: I tried Drive uploads/browser paste before checking memory for established Apps Script workflow
- Updated TOOLS.md with "Google Docs via Apps Script — STANDARD WORKFLOW" section
- Spawned runbook-builder sub-agent to create RUNBOOKS.md
- **BLOCKED: Cannot send local PDF via Telegram** — `message` tool rejects all local paths. Tried /tmp, ~/clawd/uploads/, workspace paths — all denied. Need `mediaLocalRoots` config or alternative delivery.
- **TODO**: Still need to update blueprint with OpenClaw 2026.2.12 security section, regenerate PDF, and find delivery method
- GitHub raw link fallback: https://github.com/dwayne-ubcg/solveworks/blob/main/proposals/revaly-security-blueprint.pdf

### Telegram mediaLocalRoots Discovery
- BlueBubbles docs mention `mediaLocalRoots` config for allowing local file sends
- Telegram channel likely needs similar config — check if `channels.telegram.mediaLocalRoots` exists in config schema
